title: Mercury: Ultra-Fast Language Models Based on Diffusion
authors: Samar Khanna, Siddhant Kharbanda et al. (Inception Labs)
description: First commercial diffusion LLM; Mercury Coder Mini hits 1,109 tok/s on H100, 10x faster than frontier AR models. Mercury 2 (Feb 2026) adds reasoning at 1,009 tok/s on Blackwell.

title: Mamba-3
authors: Aakash Lahoti, Kevin Y. Li, Berlin Chen et al. (CMU/Princeton)
description: ICLR 2026 Oral. Three innovations (exponential-trapezoidal discretization, complex-valued SSM, MIMO) advance SSM Pareto frontier; +1.8 pp avg accuracy over Mamba-2 with half the state size.

title: LLaDA: Large Language Diffusion with mAsking
description: First 8B-scale diffusion language model from scratch; competitive with LLaMA3-8B on ICL; addresses reversal curse. LLaDA2.0 (2512.15745) scales to 16B/100B MoE.

title: KVzip: KV Cache Compression with Query-Agnostic Eviction
authors: Jang-Hyun Kim et al. (Seoul National University)
description: NeurIPS 2025 Oral. 3-4x KV cache reduction, 2x FlashAttention decoding speedup using LLM-based importance scoring; reusable across diverse queries.

title: SageAttention3: First FP4 Attention Kernel
description: NeurIPS 2025 Spotlight. 1,038 TOPS on RTX 5090 -- 5x speedup over FlashAttention on same GPU; first trainable 8-bit attention (SageBwd) with 1.67x training speedup.

title: FlashAttention-4: Hardware-Aware Attention for Blackwell GPUs
authors: Ted Zadouri, Markus Hoehnerbach, Jay Shah, Tri Dao et al.
description: MLSys 2026. 1,613 TFLOPs/s (71% utilization) on B200 Blackwell; 1.3x over cuDNN, 2.7x over Triton; implemented in CuTe-DSL (20-30x faster compile).

title: The AI Scaling Wall of Diminishing Returns
description: Each decade of additional compute yields only ~1-2 pp MMLU-Pro improvement; scaling curve asymptotes near 95%.

title: The Wall Confronting Large Language Models
description: Scaling law exponents are below 0.1 -- regime of strongly diminishing returns; tension between learning power and accuracy as potential root cause.

title: Reasoning Models Can Be Effective Without Thinking
description: NoThinking prompting outperforms Thinking on 7 reasoning datasets in low-budget settings (51.3 vs 28.9 on ACM23 at 700 tokens); parallel NoThinking matches Thinking with 9x less latency.

title: The Illusion of Thinking
description: LRMs show complete accuracy collapse beyond complexity thresholds; standard LLMs outperform LRMs at low complexity; spawned 2 rebuttal papers and a replication study.
note: Apple paper; major Twitter/HN debate; 3+ response papers.

title: Do Thinking Tokens Help or Trap?
description: Identifies thinking trap -- thinking tokens trigger unnecessary high-level reasoning on simple tasks, reducing efficiency.

title: Not All Tokens Are What You Need In Thinking
description: Documents redundant thinking -- reasoning token consumption doesn't decrease with compression; models generate overly elaborate steps.

title: Rethinking Thinking Tokens: LLMs as Improvement Operators
description: Parallel-then-Distill-then-Refine (PDR): iteration with short contexts + compact summaries substitutes for long traces; +11% AIME 2024, +9% AIME 2025.

title: Breaking Myths in LLM Scaling
description: Statistical analysis across many models reveals architecture and training type matter as much as raw scale.

title: CommVQ: Commutative Vector Quantization for KV Cache Compression
description: ICML 2025, learned VQ SOTA (1-2 bit KV).

title: JanusQuant: Accurate and Efficient 2-bit KV Cache Quantization
BitDecoding: Unlocking Tensor Cores for Long-Context Decoding
description: HPCA 2026, low-bit KV decoding unlocking Tensor Cores.

title: XQuant: Ultra-Low Bit KV Cache with Cross-Layer Compression
